Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: BOM validation checks that each component complies with the required regulation (e.g., RoHS).
Foreign key reference to the component material or part from the SKU master data that is required for this assembly. Each BOM line references exactly one component SKU.
Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: BOM Supplier Allocation process tracks which vendor supplies each component, essential for production scheduling and quality audits.
Identifier of the work center or production resource where this component is consumed. Used for shop floor material staging and kitting.
Identifier for a group of interchangeable components that can substitute for each other in the assembly. Used for flexible BOM structures and supply chain resilience.
Priority ranking for this component within its alternative item group. Lower numbers indicate higher priority for material selection during MRP and production execution.

The hierarchical level of this component in the multi-level BOM structure. Level 0 is the finished product, level 1 is direct sub-assembly, level 2 is sub-sub-assembly, etc. Used for BOM explosion and implosion.

Flag indicating whether this component is consumed automatically via backflushing when the operation is confirmed (True) or requires manual goods issue (False). Used in lean manufacturing and MES integration.
Flag indicating whether this component is a bulk material (liquids, powders, gases) that requires special handling, storage, and measurement during production.
Internal change control number tracking modifications to this BOM line. Used for audit trail and version control in engineering change management.
Textual description of the component part or material used in the assembly, providing additional context beyond the SKU reference.
Height dimension of the component in millimeters. Used for variable-size materials and spatial planning in assembly.
Length dimension of the component in millimeters. Used for variable-size materials and spatial planning in assembly.
Procurement strategy for this component: make (manufactured in-house), buy (purchased from supplier), make_or_buy (flexible sourcing), subcontract (outsourced manufacturing).
Weight of the component in kilograms. Used for calculating total assembly weight, shipping weight, and material handling requirements.
Width dimension of the component in millimeters. Used for variable-size materials and spatial planning in assembly.
Flag indicating whether this component should be included in product costing calculations. Text items and phantom assemblies are typically not cost-relevant.
User ID or name of the person who created this BOM line record. Used for accountability and audit trail.
Date and time when this BOM line record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Indicates whether this component is critical for production and requires special attention in material planning, procurement, and availability checking.
Reference to the Engineering Change Notice that authorized this BOM line change. Provides traceability to engineering change documentation.
Reference to the Engineering Change Order that introduced or modified this BOM line. Links BOM changes to formal engineering change management processes.
The date after which this BOM line is no longer active and should not be used in new production orders. Null indicates no planned end date.
The date from which this BOM line becomes active and should be used in production planning and execution. Supports date-effectivity for phased component changes.
Indicates whether the component quantity is fixed regardless of parent assembly batch size (True) or scales proportionally with batch size (False). Used for setup materials and consumables.
Physical location or position identifier where this component is installed in the parent assembly. Used for assembly instructions and service documentation.

Classification of the BOM line item type: stock (regular inventory item), non_stock (direct procurement), phantom (pass-through assembly with no inventory), text (documentation only), variable_size (length/weight variable), co_product (joint output), by_product (secondary output). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: stock|non_stock|phantom|text|variable_size|co_product|by_product — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Number of days before the parent assembly start date that this component must be available. Used for backward scheduling in production planning and MRP runs.
Sequential line item number within the BOM header, used for ordering and referencing specific components in the assembly structure.
User ID or name of the person who last modified this BOM line record. Used for accountability and audit trail.
Date and time when this BOM line record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit trail.
Free-text field for additional instructions, comments, or special handling requirements for this component in the assembly process.
The routing operation number at which this component is consumed or installed in the manufacturing process. Links BOM to routing for integrated production planning.

Current lifecycle status of this BOM line: active (in use), inactive (temporarily disabled), pending (awaiting approval), obsolete (phased out), blocked (quality hold), released (approved for production).
The required quantity of this component needed to produce one unit of the parent assembly. Supports up to 6 decimal places for precision in chemical, pharmaceutical, and electronics manufacturing.
Engineering revision level or version of this BOM line. Tracks design iterations and ensures correct component versions are used in production.
Expected scrap or waste percentage for this component during the manufacturing process. Used by MRP to calculate additional material requirements. Value between 0.00 and 100.00.
Indicates whether this component is also available as a spare part for after-sales service and maintenance. Used for service parts planning and documentation.

The unit of measure for the component quantity (e.g., EA=Each, PC=Piece, KG=Kilogram, L=Liter, M=Meter). Must align with the component SKU base unit of measure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|PC|KG|G|L|ML|M|CM|FT|IN|LB|OZ|SET|BOX|ROLL|SHEET — 16 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Unique identifier for the product specification record. Primary key.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: REQUIRED: Specification approval workflow tracks which engineer approved each spec via approved_by_employee_id.
Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Specification approval workflow requires linking each specification to the governing regulatory standard.
Reference to the parent product in the product catalog. Links this specification to the master product record.
Maximum operating altitude in meters above sea level. Defines altitude limitations for product operation due to air pressure and cooling considerations.
Intended application areas and use cases. Describes the industrial applications, environments, or processes for which the product is designed and suitable.
Date when this specification version was formally approved. Tracks the approval milestone in the specification lifecycle.
Product color and surface finish. Describes the external appearance and coating (e.g., RAL 7035 light grey, powder coated, anodized aluminum).
Supported communication protocols for data exchange. Lists the industrial communication standards the product supports (e.g., PROFINET, EtherNet/IP, Modbus TCP, OPC UA).
Electrical connection or terminal type. Describes the method for connecting wires or cables to the product (e.g., screw terminals, spring clamp, plug connector).
Maximum continuous current rating in amperes. Defines the electrical current capacity of the product.
Reference identifier or document number for the associated product datasheet. Links to the detailed technical datasheet managed in the engineering document management system.
Product height dimension in millimeters. Defines the physical height for installation planning and space requirements.
Product length dimension in millimeters. Defines the physical length for installation planning and space requirements.
Product width dimension in millimeters. Defines the physical width for installation planning and space requirements.
Date when this specification version becomes effective and applicable for use in engineering, manufacturing, and sales processes.
Date when this specification version is no longer valid or has been superseded by a newer revision. Null for active specifications without planned obsolescence.
Operating frequency rating in Hertz. Specifies the electrical frequency the product is designed for (e.g., 50Hz, 60Hz, 50/60Hz).
Relative humidity rating or range for product operation. Specifies the acceptable humidity conditions (e.g., 5-95% RH non-condensing).
Reference identifier or document number for the installation manual. Links to the installation instructions managed in the engineering document management system.
Ingress Protection rating per IEC 60529 standard. Defines the level of protection against solid objects and liquids (e.g., IP65, IP67, IP20).
Primary materials used in product construction. Lists the key materials and their properties (e.g., stainless steel housing, polycarbonate cover, copper conductors).
Physical mounting method for the product. Specifies how the product is installed (e.g., DIN rail, panel mount, wall mount).
NEMA enclosure rating for North American markets. Defines environmental protection level (e.g., NEMA1, NEMA4, NEMA4X, NEMA12).
Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the specification. Captures supplementary information for engineering, quality, or sales teams.
Maximum operating ambient temperature in degrees Celsius. Defines the upper temperature limit for safe and reliable product operation.
Minimum operating ambient temperature in degrees Celsius. Defines the lower temperature limit for safe and reliable product operation.
Key performance parameters and metrics. Describes critical performance characteristics such as response time, accuracy, throughput, or efficiency ratings specific to the product type.
Electrical power rating in watts. Specifies the maximum power consumption or output capacity of the product.
Timestamp when this specification record was first created in the system. Tracks the initial capture of the specification data.
Timestamp when this specification record was last modified. Tracks the most recent update to any field in the specification record.
Version or revision identifier for the specification document. Tracks changes and updates over time.
Reference identifier for the Safety Data Sheet. Links to the SDS document for products containing hazardous materials, managed in the engineering document management system.
Mechanical shock resistance specification. Describes the products ability to withstand mechanical shock per applicable test standards (e.g., IEC 60068-2-27).
Unique identifier of this specification record in the source system. Enables traceability back to the originating system for data lineage and reconciliation.
Detailed textual description of the product specification. Provides comprehensive technical and commercial details not captured in structured fields.
Human-readable name or title of the product specification. Describes the specification purpose or scope.
Unique business identifier for the product specification document. Used for external reference and traceability across engineering, quality, and sales teams.
Current lifecycle status of the product specification. Tracks the approval and active state of the specification document.
Classification of the specification document type. Indicates whether the specification covers technical parameters, commercial terms, or a combination.
Maximum storage temperature in degrees Celsius. Defines the upper temperature limit for product storage when not in operation.
Minimum storage temperature in degrees Celsius. Defines the lower temperature limit for product storage when not in operation.
Reference identifier for associated technical drawings. Links to CAD drawings, dimensional drawings, or assembly drawings managed in the PLM system.
Vibration resistance specification. Describes the products ability to withstand mechanical vibration per applicable test standards (e.g., IEC 60068-2-6).
Electrical voltage rating or range for the product. Specifies the nominal operating voltage and acceptable voltage range (e.g., 24VDC, 110-240VAC, 400V 3-phase).
Product weight in kilograms. Defines the mass for shipping, handling, and installation planning.
Primary key for change_order
Name of the person assigned as the change owner responsible for driving the change through approval and implementation. Accountable for successful execution.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: REQUIRED: Change order initiation logs initiator_employee_id to identify the employee who raised the change.
Foreign key linking to product.product_revision. Business justification: Change orders often target a specific product revision; linking change_order to product_revision captures this ownership.
Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Tie each change order to the primary SKU it affects; removes unstructured affected_sku_list column
Indicates which level(s) of the BOM hierarchy are impacted by the change: top-level finished goods, sub-assemblies, individual components, raw materials, or all levels.
Date when the change order received final approval from all required approvers and was authorized for implementation.
Overall approval decision status across all required approvers. Conditional indicates approval with stipulations; escalated indicates management review required.

Current stage in the multi-level approval workflow. Indicates which functional area is currently reviewing the change order. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: engineering|quality|manufacturing|procurement|finance|executive|complete — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Comprehensive description of the proposed change, detailing what is being modified, the scope of the change, and the technical nature of the modification.
Standardized code indicating the primary business driver for the change: quality improvement, cost reduction, regulatory compliance, component obsolescence, customer request, or safety enhancement.
Detailed narrative explanation of why the change is being requested, including background context, problem statement, and business justification.

business_glossary_term: Engineering Change Order (ECO) / Engineering Change Notice (ECN) Request Number
description: The externally-known unique identifier for the change order, typically formatted as ECO-NNNNNN or ECN-NNNNNN. This is the business identifier used across PLM, ERP, and MES systems.

Current lifecycle status of the change order in the approval and implementation workflow. Tracks progression from initial draft through review, approval, implementation, and closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|rejected|implemented|closed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Classification of the change order by the nature of the modification: design (product design changes), process (manufacturing process changes), documentation (drawing/spec updates), material (material substitution), specification (technical spec changes), or BOM (bill of materials structure changes).
Date when the change order was formally closed after successful implementation and validation. Marks the end of the change lifecycle.
Timestamp when the change order record was first created in the PLM system. Marks the beginning of the change request lifecycle.
Status of customer approval for the change, if customer notification is required. Tracks whether the customer has reviewed and approved the proposed modification.
Indicates whether customer notification is required for this change. True for changes that affect form, fit, function, or regulatory compliance requiring customer approval or notification.
The date on which the change becomes effective and must be implemented in production. This is the cutover date for transitioning from old to new specifications.
Optional serial number or production lot number at which the change becomes effective. Used for mid-production changes where effectivity is tied to specific units rather than a date.
Estimated financial impact of implementing the change, including tooling, material, labor, and rework costs. Used for cost-benefit analysis and approval decision-making.
Assessment of the impact on existing inventory, including obsolete stock quantities, rework requirements, scrap decisions, and transition inventory management strategy.
Estimated impact on product lead time resulting from the change, measured in days. Positive values indicate lead time increase; negative values indicate reduction.
Assessment of quality implications, including changes to inspection requirements, test procedures, quality control points, and expected quality improvements or risks.
Actual date when the change was fully implemented in production and the new specifications became active.
Status of the physical implementation of the change in production. Tracks execution progress after approval has been granted.
Code identifying the manufacturing plant or facility responsible for implementing the change. May be a single plant or multiple plants for multi-site rollout.
Department or functional area of the change initiator (e.g., Engineering, Quality, Manufacturing, Procurement).
Timestamp of the most recent update to the change order record. Tracks the latest modification for audit and synchronization purposes.
Free-form text field for additional comments, implementation notes, lessons learned, or other relevant information not captured in structured fields.
Unique identifier for this change order in the source PLM system (Siemens Teamcenter). Used for system integration and traceability back to the system of record.
The product revision level that existed before this change order. Provides traceability to the prior design state.
Business priority level for processing and implementing the change. Critical priority indicates safety or regulatory urgency requiring immediate action.
Name of the regulatory authority or certification body whose requirements are impacted by this change (e.g., UL, IEC, OSHA, EPA, CE). Applicable when regulatory_impact_flag is true.
Indicates whether the change has regulatory compliance implications requiring additional review, testing, or certification updates (e.g., UL, CE, safety certifications).

business_glossary_term: Related Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) Number
description: Reference to the CAPA record associated with this change order. Links the change to corrective or preventive action initiatives.
Reference to the Non-Conformance Report that triggered this change order, if applicable. Links the change to quality issues or defects that necessitated the modification.
The new product revision level resulting from this change order. Tracks version progression of the product design (e.g., Rev A, Rev B, Rev 01, Rev 02).
Boolean indicator for expedited processing requirements. True indicates the change requires fast-track approval and implementation due to safety, regulatory, or critical business needs.
Date when validation testing was completed and results were documented. Marks the final verification of the change implementation.
Indicates whether post-implementation validation testing is required to verify the change achieved its intended results without introducing new issues.
Status of post-implementation validation activities. Tracks whether the change has been verified to meet requirements and quality standards.
Unique identifier for the product certification record. Primary key.

Reference to the manufactured product, automation system, electrification solution, or smart infrastructure component that holds this certification.

Technical standards, regulations, or directives against which the product was certified (e.g., IEC 61131-2, EN 60204-1, UL 508). Pipe-separated list of standard identifiers.

Name of the organization or authority that issued the certification (e.g., Underwriters Laboratories, TUV Rheinland, Notified Body, National Standards Authority).
Total cost incurred to obtain and maintain this certification, including testing fees, audit fees, and certification body charges.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the certification cost amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).
Timestamp when this certification record was first created in the system.
Cybersecurity certification or compliance level achieved by the product (e.g., IEC 62443-4-1, IEC 62443-4-2, NIST Cybersecurity Framework).
Unique identifier for the manufacturer's Declaration of Conformity document associated with this certification, particularly for CE marking.
Export Control Classification Number assigned under the U.S. Commerce Control List for dual-use items subject to export regulations.
Date from which the certification becomes valid and the product is authorized for market access under the certification scope.
Energy efficiency classification or rating assigned to the product (e.g., Energy Star, EU Energy Label class A+++, IEC 60034-30-1 efficiency class IE3).
Environmental management or sustainability certification obtained (e.g., ISO 14001, LEED, BREEAM, Cradle to Cradle).
Date on which the certification expires and requires renewal. Null for certifications with indefinite validity subject to ongoing compliance.
Functional safety certification level achieved by the product (e.g., IEC 61508 SIL 2, ISO 13849 PLd, IEC 61511).
Indicates whether this certification is typically disclosed to customers in product documentation, datasheets, or sales materials as a market differentiator.

Timestamp when this certification record was last modified or updated in the system.
Scheduled date for the next surveillance audit or re-certification assessment by the certifying body.
Description of the product models, configurations, or variants covered under this certification. Defines the boundary of what is certified.
Quality management system certification under which the product was manufactured (e.g., ISO 9001, IATF 16949, AS9100).

Target or scheduled date for certification renewal or re-assessment to maintain continuous compliance.
Indicates whether the product complies with RoHS Directive restricting the use of hazardous substances in electrical and electronic equipment.
Reference number of the test report or assessment documentation that supports the certification issuance.

business_glossary_term: Waste Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE) Compliant
description: Indicates whether the product complies with WEEE Directive for proper disposal and recycling of electronic waste.

Current lifecycle status of the product revision indicating its approval and release state.
Unique identifier of this revision record in the source PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) system for traceability and reconciliation.
Indicates whether this revision includes changes to product specifications, performance requirements, or acceptance criteria.

Indicates whether this revision requires changes to manufacturing tooling, fixtures, jigs, or molds.

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Bundle profitability analysis aggregates revenue and cost under a dedicated profit center.
Current approval status for new or modified bundles requiring management or product committee approval before activation.
Timestamp when this bundle was approved for sale. Null if approval_status is not 'approved'.
Indicates whether the bundle requires factory assembly or integration before shipment (True) or is shipped as separate components (False).
Date when this bundle is no longer available for new orders. Null for ongoing offerings. Used for promotional bundles and product phase-out planning.
Date when this bundle becomes available for sale. Used for new product introduction (NPI) and promotional campaign timing.
Externally-known unique business identifier for the bundle, used in sales orders and quotations. Typically follows company SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) pattern for composite offerings.
Detailed commercial description of the bundle, including key features, benefits, and typical use cases. Used in catalogs, quotations, and sales documentation.
Human-readable commercial name of the bundle as presented to customers and used in sales materials (e.g., 'Automation Cell Starter Kit', 'Smart Factory Solution Package').
Current lifecycle state of the bundle: draft (under development), active (available for sale), inactive (temporarily unavailable), discontinued (no longer sold but supported), obsolete (end-of-life).

Classification of the bundle structure: fixed (pre-defined components, no substitution), configurable (customer can select options), promotional (time-limited marketing offer), solution (engineered system package), service (includes maintenance/support).
List of industry certifications or compliance standards the bundle meets (e.g., 'CE', 'UL', 'IEC 61508', 'ATEX'). Critical for regulated industries and export compliance.
Total number of distinct SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) components included in this bundle. Used for complexity assessment and inventory planning.

Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the primary country of manufacture or assembly for the bundle. Used for customs, tariffs, and trade compliance.
User identifier or name of the person who created this bundle record. Used for accountability and audit purposes.
Timestamp when this bundle record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the list price (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).
Standard discount percentage applied to the sum-of-parts price when pricing_method is 'discounted'. Null for other pricing methods.
Code identifying the distribution channel through which this bundle is sold (e.g., direct sales, distributor, OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer), e-commerce).
Export Control Classification Number assigned to the bundle for U.S. export control compliance. Determines licensing requirements for international shipments.
Energy efficiency classification or rating for the bundle if applicable (e.g., 'A+++', 'Energy Star', 'IE4'). Used for sustainability reporting and customer decision-making.
Environmental compliance certifications and declarations for the bundle (e.g., 'RoHS', 'REACH', 'WEEE', 'ISO 14001'). Critical for EU and global environmental regulations.
Hazardous material classification code if the bundle contains components subject to dangerous goods regulations. Used for shipping and handling compliance.
Harmonized System tariff classification code for the bundle. Used for international trade, customs declarations, and duty calculation.
URL to the primary product image or rendering of the bundle. Used in e-commerce, catalogs, and sales presentations.
User identifier or name of the person who last modified this bundle record. Used for change management and audit trail.
Timestamp when this bundle record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.
Standard lead time in days from order placement to delivery for this bundle. Accounts for component availability, assembly, and logistics.
Standard list price for the bundle in the base currency. Represents the published price before any customer-specific discounts or negotiations.
Extended marketing copy and value proposition for the bundle, used in promotional materials, web catalogs, and sales presentations. Distinct from technical bundle_description.
Minimum number of bundle units that must be ordered in a single transaction. Used to enforce volume thresholds for bundled offerings.
Description of the standard packaging method for the bundle (e.g., 'Wooden Crate', 'Cardboard Box', 'Pallet', 'Custom Container'). Impacts shipping and handling requirements.

Method used to calculate bundle price: sum_of_parts (total of component prices), fixed_price (single bundle price regardless of components), discounted (percentage off sum-of-parts), tiered (volume-based pricing), custom (negotiated per deal).
Bundle code of the successor or replacement bundle when this bundle is discontinued. Used for product transition planning and customer migration.
Code identifying the sales organization responsible for this bundle. Used in multi-division enterprises to segment product portfolios by business unit or geography.
Code identifying the standard service level or SLA (Service Level Agreement) tier included with the bundle (e.g., 'BASIC', 'PREMIUM', '24x7').
Primary market segment or industry vertical this bundle is designed for (e.g., 'Automotive Manufacturing', 'Food & Beverage', 'Pharmaceutical', 'Discrete Manufacturing').
URL or document reference to the technical documentation, installation guides, and specifications for the bundle. Used by sales engineering and customer support.
Total shipping volume of the complete bundle in cubic meters, including packaging. Used for warehouse space planning and transportation optimization.
Standard warranty period in months provided with the bundle. Covers all included components under a unified warranty policy.
Total shipping weight of the complete bundle in kilograms, including all components and packaging. Used for logistics planning and freight cost calculation.

Reference to the product manager responsible for this catalog entry. Supports product ownership and accountability.
Reference to the catalog entry that replaces this product when it is discontinued or superseded. Supports product transition management and customer migration.
Reference to the engineering SKU master data. Links the commercial catalog entry to the underlying product master record managed in SAP S/4HANA MM and Siemens Teamcenter PLM.
Detailed commercial description of the product offering including key features, benefits, and applications. Used in sales literature and customer communications.
URL reference to the primary product image for this catalog entry. Used in online catalogs, e-commerce platforms, and sales presentations.
The commercial product name as it appears in sales catalogs, quotations, and customer-facing documentation. May differ from the engineering product name.
The externally-facing commercial catalog identifier used in sales orders, quotations, and customer communications. This is the orderable product code visible to customers and distributors.
Current lifecycle status of the catalog entry indicating whether the product is available for sale, pending launch, or being phased out.
Version identifier for the product catalog publication (e.g., 2024.1, 2024.2). Supports catalog change management and historical tracking.
Comma-separated list of certification marks and compliance logos applicable to this catalog entry (e.g., UL, CE, CSA, IEC). Used for regulatory compliance and customer requirements.
Indicates whether this catalog entry supports customer-specific configuration or customization (e.g., programmable controllers, modular systems with selectable options).
Three-letter ISO country code indicating the country where this product is manufactured or substantially transformed. Required for customs declarations and trade compliance.
The timestamp when this catalog entry record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the list price (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).
The distribution chain identifier combining sales organization, distribution channel, and division. Used for SAP SD integration and order processing.
The date after which this catalog entry is no longer available for new orders. Null for products with indefinite availability. Used for planned product discontinuations.
The date from which this catalog entry becomes available for sale and order entry. Supports time-based product launches and regional rollouts.
Environmental compliance certifications and declarations for this catalog entry (e.g., RoHS, REACH, WEEE). Required for sales in regulated markets.
Export control classification for this catalog entry under applicable trade regulations (e.g., US ECCN, EU dual-use codes). Required for international sales compliance.
International harmonized commodity code for customs and tariff classification. Used for import/export documentation and duty calculation.
Indicates whether this catalog entry contains or is classified as hazardous material requiring special handling, shipping, and regulatory compliance.
The timestamp when this catalog entry record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.
The date when the list price for this catalog entry was last updated. Used for price change tracking and audit trails.
Current stage in the product lifecycle from New Product Introduction (NPI) through end-of-life. Used for portfolio management and strategic planning.
The standard list price for this catalog entry in the base currency. Actual transaction prices may vary based on customer agreements, volume discounts, and promotions.
The minimum quantity that must be ordered for this catalog entry. Used to enforce business rules for small-volume orders and manufacturing lot sizes.
The user ID or username of the person who last modified this catalog entry record. Used for audit trails and accountability.

Free-text description of any limitations or restrictions on where the substitute can be used. May reference specific customer segments, geographic regions, or application types.
Date when the substitution was formally approved by engineering, quality, or product management.
Indicates whether the substitution can be applied automatically by MRP or order management systems (true) or requires manual approval (false).
Indicates whether the substitute product requires additional compliance certifications (UL, CE, IEC) before it can be used in regulated applications.
Timestamp when the substitution record was first created in the system.
Indicates whether customer approval or notification is required before applying the substitution in order fulfillment.

Date when the substitution becomes valid and can be used in order management, MRP, and customer service processes.
Date when the substitution is no longer valid. Null indicates indefinite validity.

Indicates the degree of form-fit-function equivalence between primary and substitute. Exact means identical in all dimensions; partial means some differences exist; none means significant differences.
Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where this substitution is NOT permitted due to regulatory, certification, or business policy reasons.
Indicates whether the substitution is bidirectional (true) or unidirectional (false). When true, both SKUs can substitute for each other.
Name or identifier of the user or system that last modified the substitution record.
Timestamp when the substitution record was last updated.
Most recent date when this substitution was applied in a business transaction.
Difference in standard lead time between substitute and primary SKU, measured in days. Positive values indicate longer lead time for substitute.
Percentage difference in list price between the substitute and primary SKU. Positive values indicate the substitute is more expensive; negative values indicate cost savings.
Numeric ranking indicating preference order when multiple substitutes exist for the same primary SKU. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used by MRP and order management fallback logic.
Indicates whether quality assurance has verified that the substitute meets the same quality standards as the primary SKU through testing or certification.

Business reason for establishing the substitution relationship. Captures why the primary SKU requires a substitute. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: discontinued|end_of_life|allocation|supply_shortage|obsolete|cost_reduction|quality_improvement|design_change — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Unique identifier of this substitution record in the source system of record.
The replacement or substitute SKU that can be used in place of the primary SKU.

Current lifecycle status of the substitution record. Active indicates the substitution is in effect; pending approval awaits engineering or quality review; approved is authorized but not yet effective; rejected was not approved; expired is no longer valid; suspended is temporarily inactive.

Classification of the substitution relationship. Direct replacement indicates identical form-fit-function; functional equivalent provides same capability with potential differences; upward compatible offers enhanced features; temporary substitute is short-term solution; cross-reference indicates OEM equivalence; alternate is approved alternative.
Engineering or technical notes describing differences between primary and substitute SKUs, installation considerations, or compatibility information.
Number of times this substitution has been applied in order management or MRP processes. Used for analytics and substitution effectiveness tracking.

business_glossary_term: Device Contract Assignment - Sales Contract Id
description: Foreign key linking to the sales contract
Business attribute for device_contract_assignment.
Business attribute for device_contract_assignment.
Date when the device coverage under the contract ends
Date when the device coverage under the contract starts
Service level agreed for the device under the contract
Primary key for forecast
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Sales forecasts feed cost center budgets; linking ensures accurate cost planning.

Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Forecast contains a free‑text territory field; adding forecast_territory_id creates a normalized link to sales.territory and eliminates the duplicate string column.
Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Sales forecast per material drives production scheduling and inventory replenishment decisions.
Reference to the sales opportunity this forecast is associated with.
Reference to the prior version of this forecast if this is a revision.
Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Forecasted profit per profit center is required for quarterly performance dashboards.
Sales representative, manager, or regional leader who submitted this forecast.
Date when the forecast was approved by management.
Manager or executive who approved this forecast submission.
Optimistic revenue projection if all opportunities close favorably.
Actual revenue from deals already closed and won during the forecast period.
High-confidence revenue projection that the sales team commits to achieving.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the primary market of this forecast.
Date when the forecast record was first created in the system.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all forecast amounts.
End date of the forecast period being projected.
Fiscal quarter designation (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4) for the forecast period.
Fiscal year for the forecast period.
Classification of forecast confidence level: pipeline (early stage), best_case (optimistic), commit (high confidence), closed (won deals).
Business identifier for the forecast record, typically system-generated.
Current lifecycle status of the forecast record.
Target industry vertical for the forecasted opportunities (e.g., Automotive, Energy, Pharmaceuticals).
Flag indicating whether this is the most current version of the forecast for this period and owner.
User who last modified this forecast record.
Date when the forecast record was last updated.
Revenue amount after manager review and adjustment, may differ from submitted amount based on pipeline scrutiny.
Commentary from the reviewing manager explaining adjustments or concerns.
Free-text commentary from the sales representative explaining assumptions, risks, or opportunities in the forecast.
Number of opportunities included in this forecast submission.
Time granularity of the forecast period: monthly, quarterly, or annual.
Total pipeline revenue including early-stage opportunities with lower probability.
Primary product line or business unit for this forecast (e.g., Industrial Automation, Electrification, Smart Infrastructure).
Assigned sales quota target for the forecast owner during this period.
Percentage of quota achieved based on committed forecast amount.
Broader geographic region grouping multiple territories (e.g., EMEA, Americas, APAC).
Explanation provided when a forecast is rejected by management, requiring revision.
Start date of the forecast period being projected.
Date when the forecast was submitted by the sales representative or manager.
Precise timestamp when the forecast was submitted into the system.
Revenue amount submitted by the sales representative for this forecast period and category.
Difference between committed forecast amount and quota target, can be positive or negative.
Version number tracking revisions to the forecast for the same period.
Pipeline amount adjusted by probability weighting based on opportunity stages.
Primary key for order_intake
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Revenue allocation to cost center for cost accounting on each order intake (required for statutory cost reporting).
Reference to the customer account placing the order. Links to the master customer account record.
Foreign key linking to automation.device_registry. Business justification: Order intake must reference the physical device to be commissioned; required for asset handoff and installation scheduling.

Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_register. Business justification: Allows Order Intake to reference the exact equipment to be delivered, essential for logistics, installation planning, and invoicing.
Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Order intake records the exact material to be delivered, linking to inventory for allocation and picking.
Reference to the won sales opportunity that generated this order intake. Links to the originating opportunity record in Salesforce Sales Cloud.

Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Order intake records a sales territory as text; adding order_intake_territory_id creates a proper FK to sales.territory and removes the ambiguous string column.
Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability reporting per profit center for each order intake (used in profit analysis reports).
Reference to the customer project or program this order is associated with. Used for project-based revenue tracking and multi-phase delivery coordination.
Reference to the accepted quote that was converted into this order intake. May be null if order originated directly from opportunity without formal quote.
Reference to the master sales contract or framework agreement under which this order was placed. May be null for spot orders without framework agreement.
Reference to the sales representative or account manager who owns this order intake. Used for quota attainment and commission calculation.
Reference to the sales team responsible for this order. Used for team-based performance tracking and collaborative selling analytics.
Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Order intake processing must map incoming orders to the master product record for inventory allocation and production scheduling.

Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Order fulfillment planning requires assigning a primary supplier to each order intake for procurement scheduling and delivery coordination.

Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_site. Business justification: Logistics tracking needs the specific supplier site fulfilling the order, enabling shipping, compliance, and performance reporting.
The date when this order intake was recognized as a booking for quota and sales performance measurement. May differ from intake date based on validation rules.
Indicates whether this order intake has been recognized as a booking against sales quota. True when order meets booking recognition criteria per sales policy.
The delivery date committed by sales to the customer. May differ from requested date based on production capacity and supply chain constraints.
System timestamp when this order intake record was first created. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Date when credit approval was granted for this order, if required. Null if order did not require credit approval or is still pending.
Result of the customer credit check performed at order intake. Orders failing credit check may require advance payment or credit approval before handoff to fulfillment.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the order value. Essential for multi-currency sales operations and financial consolidation.
The date on the customer's purchase order. Used for contract compliance verification and order validity checks.
The customer's purchase order reference number. Critical for order validation, invoicing, and customer reconciliation. May be required per customer contract terms.
The customer's delivery address or site location for this order. May reference a customer site ID or contain full address details.
The foreign exchange rate applied at intake date to convert order value to base currency. Locked at intake for consistent booking valuation.
The fiscal quarter in which this order intake was booked. Format Q1-YYYY through Q4-YYYY. Used for quarterly sales performance reporting and quota tracking.
The fiscal year in which this order intake was booked. Used for annual sales performance reporting and year-over-year growth analysis.
The date when the order intake was successfully handed off to the order management domain. Used for sales-to-fulfillment cycle time KPI calculation.
Current status of the handoff from sales to order management domain. Tracks the intake record through validation, transfer, and acceptance by the fulfillment organization.

The Incoterms rule defining the division of costs and risks between buyer and seller. Critical for international trade compliance and logistics planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The customer's industry segment or vertical market. Used for market analysis, sales territory planning, and industry-specific KPI tracking.
The date when the order was officially received and recorded in the sales system. This is the booking date used for sales quota recognition and revenue pipeline tracking.
Business identifier for the order intake record. Human-readable unique number used for tracking and reference in sales reporting and KPI dashboards.
Precise timestamp when the order intake record was created in the system. Used for sales-to-fulfillment cycle time measurement and operational analytics.
Username or identifier of the user who last modified this order intake record.
System timestamp when this order intake record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.
Free-text notes and comments related to this order intake. May include customer requests, internal coordination notes, or special handling instructions.
The order number or reference assigned by the order management system after handoff. Links this sales intake record to the authoritative order record in SAP SD or equivalent ERP.
The priority level assigned to this order intake. Influences production scheduling and resource allocation in the fulfillment process.

Classification of the order by business type. Distinguishes between new customer acquisition, existing customer expansion, and service orders for sales mix analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: New Business|Renewal|Upgrade|Add-On|Replacement|Service|Spare Parts — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The total confirmed order value at intake. This is the gross order amount before any adjustments, used for sales booking recognition and quota attainment calculation.
Order value converted to the company's base reporting currency using the exchange rate at intake date. Used for consolidated sales reporting and KPI tracking.

The agreed payment terms for this order. Defines when payment is due relative to delivery or invoice date. Critical for cash flow forecasting and credit risk management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Net 30|Net 45|Net 60|Net 90|Advance Payment|Letter of Credit|Installment|Custom — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Number of days until payment is due per the agreed payment terms. Used for accounts receivable forecasting and working capital planning.
The primary product line or business unit for this order. Used for sales performance segmentation and product portfolio analysis.
The customer's requested delivery date as captured at order intake. Used for production planning coordination and customer expectation management.

The agreed shipping or transportation method for order delivery. Impacts delivery lead time and logistics cost allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Air Freight|Sea Freight|Road Transport|Rail|Courier|Customer Pickup|Multimodal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Any special commercial terms, conditions, or customer-specific agreements applicable to this order. May include warranty extensions, service level commitments, or pricing exceptions.
Username or identifier of the sales user who created this order intake record in the system.
Unique identifier for the sales quota record. Primary key.
Identifier of the parent quota in a hierarchical quota structure (e.g., a regional quota that rolls up to a global quota). Enables quota hierarchy navigation and rollup reporting.
Identifier of the user who created this quota record. Links to the user record for audit trail and accountability purposes.
Identifier of the manager or executive who approved this quota assignment. Links to the user record for audit trail and governance purposes.
Identifier of the user who most recently modified this quota record. Links to the user record for change tracking and audit compliance.

Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Quota has a free‑text territory field; adding quota_territory_id links quota to the territory hierarchy and eliminates the redundant string column.
Identifier of the sales representative, team, or territory to whom this quota is assigned. Links to the employee or organizational unit responsible for achieving the target.

The net amount by which the base quota was adjusted after initial assignment. Positive values indicate quota increases; negative values indicate reductions. Used for variance analysis and compensation dispute resolution.
The effective date of the most recent quota adjustment. Used to track when changes were applied for accurate period-over-period performance comparison.

Free-text explanation for any mid-period adjustments to the quota (e.g., territory realignment, market condition changes, organizational restructuring). Provides business context for quota modifications.

The methodology used to determine this quota amount: top_down (corporate target cascaded down), bottom_up (aggregated from territory forecasts), hybrid (combination), historical (based on prior period performance), or market_based (derived from market opportunity analysis).
The approval workflow state for this quota assignment: pending (awaiting management review), approved (confirmed and active), or rejected (not accepted, requires revision).
The date when this quota was formally approved by management. Used for audit trail and to track planning cycle timelines.
The actual performance amount achieved against this quota as of the last calculation date. Updated periodically (daily, weekly, monthly) based on closed-won opportunities or booked orders.

The percentage of base quota achieved, calculated as (attainment_amount / base_quota_amount) * 100. Key performance indicator (KPI) for sales performance dashboards and incentive compensation calculations.
The standard target amount assigned to the quota owner for the period. Represents the minimum performance expectation for full incentive payout.
Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code (e.g., USA, DEU, CHN) representing the primary country for this quota assignment. Supports country-level performance analysis and compliance reporting.
The timestamp when this quota record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and to track planning cycle execution timelines.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP) in which the quota amounts are denominated, enabling multi-currency quota management for global sales organizations.
The fiscal quarter (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4) to which this quota applies, supporting quarterly performance tracking and incentive compensation cycles.
The fiscal year to which this quota applies, enabling year-over-year performance analysis and annual planning alignment.
The target industry vertical or customer segment for this quota (e.g., Automotive, Energy, Pharmaceuticals, Infrastructure). Enables industry-focused quota planning and vertical market analysis.
Boolean flag indicating whether this quota is assigned to a team (True) or an individual sales representative (False). Supports team-based incentive structures and collaborative selling models.
The date when the attainment amount and percentage were last recalculated. Indicates the freshness of performance metrics for reporting and compensation purposes.
The timestamp when this quota record was most recently updated. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and data synchronization processes.
Free-text field for additional context, special conditions, or instructions related to this quota assignment (e.g., strategic account focus, product launch priorities, market entry considerations).
The last date of the performance period for which this quota is effective. Defines the end of the measurement window.
The first date of the performance period for which this quota is effective. Defines the beginning of the measurement window.

The product line or business unit to which this quota applies (e.g., Automation Systems, Electrification Solutions, Smart Infrastructure). Enables product-specific quota allocation and performance tracking.
Human-readable business identifier for the quota record, typically following a pattern such as QTA-YYYYNNNN for external reference and reporting.
Current lifecycle state of the quota: draft (under review), active (in effect), suspended (temporarily paused), closed (period completed), or cancelled (no longer valid).

Classification of the quota by measurement type: revenue (monetary sales target), units (volume target), margin (gross profit target), new logos (new customer acquisition target), bookings (order intake target), or pipeline (opportunity generation target).
The broader geographic region encompassing the territory (e.g., Americas, EMEA, APAC). Used for regional rollup reporting and executive dashboards.
The gap between base quota and current attainment, calculated as (base_quota_amount - attainment_amount). Indicates the remaining target to achieve full quota for the period.
The aspirational target amount beyond the base quota, used to incentivize exceptional performance with accelerated commission rates or bonuses.
Unique identifier for the sales contract. Primary key for the sales contract entity.
Foreign key linking to automation.automation_project. Business justification: Each contract defines scope for an automation project; linking enables project budgeting and compliance reporting.
Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_contract. Business justification: SHIPPING TERMS: Sales contract references specific carrier contract; needed for compliance and rate enforcement.
Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Each contract creates specific compliance obligations (reporting, testing) that must be recorded and monitored.
Foreign key linking to compliance.controlled_document. Business justification: Contracts attach compliance documents (certificates, test reports) for auditability; linking provides direct access.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Contract accounting requires mapping contract revenue/costs to a cost center for internal cost tracking.
Foreign key linking to customer.credit_profile. Business justification: Linking contracts to credit profiles enables automated credit risk checks required for contract approval in manufacturing.
Reference to the customer account that is the counterparty to this sales contract. Links to the customer master data for billing, shipping, and relationship management.
Reference to the employee or business unit that owns and is accountable for this sales contract. Responsible for contract administration, compliance, and customer relationship.

Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_register. Business justification: Contracts often cover specific equipment for service or warranty; linking provides traceability for compliance and service scheduling.
Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Contracts often cover entire product families; linking enables compliance checks, warranty terms, and pricing rules per family.
Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profit center assignment enables profit margin reporting per contract (mandatory for management reporting).
Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Contract‑Project binding: ensures the contractual obligations are tied to the specific project for compliance and cost tracking.

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Contracts embed clauses referencing the primary regulatory requirement governing the sale, enabling legal compliance tracking.
Reference to the sales representative or account manager responsible for negotiating and managing this sales contract. Links to employee or sales team master data.
Reference to the geographic or organizational sales territory to which this contract is assigned. Used for territory management, quota tracking, and commission calculation.

Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Sales contracts often reference a supplier responsible for warranty or service parts; linking enables warranty claim routing and supplier performance monitoring.
Date when the sales contract received final internal approval and was authorized for execution. Marks completion of internal review and sign-off process.

List of mandatory certifications, standards, or regulatory approvals that products or services must meet under the sales contract. Examples: CE marking, UL certification, ISO 9001, IEC 61131, OSHA compliance.

Contractual provisions protecting proprietary information, trade secrets, technical specifications, and business data shared between parties. Defines confidential information, permitted uses, and disclosure restrictions.
Externally-known unique business identifier for the sales contract. Used for customer communication, legal reference, and cross-system tracking.

Current lifecycle status of the sales contract. Draft contracts are being prepared; pending approval contracts await internal or customer sign-off; active contracts are in force; suspended contracts are temporarily on hold; completed contracts have fulfilled all obligations; terminated contracts ended early by mutual agreement; cancelled contracts were voided before activation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|active|suspended|completed|terminated|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Classification of the sales contract based on commercial structure. Standard contracts use pre-approved terms; custom contracts are negotiated; framework contracts establish terms for multiple orders; blanket contracts cover recurring purchases; spot contracts are one-time transactions; turnkey contracts include design, supply, and commissioning.
Timestamp when this sales contract record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for data lineage and compliance.
Full name of the authorized representative who signed the sales contract on behalf of the customer. Used for legal verification and audit trail.
Physical address or site where contracted products, systems, or equipment will be delivered. May reference customer facility, project site, or designated warehouse.
Detailed timeline and milestones for delivery of products, systems, or services under the sales contract. May include phased deliveries, installation schedules, and commissioning dates.

Agreed mechanism for resolving disputes arising from the sales contract. Litigation involves court proceedings; arbitration uses neutral arbitrators; mediation involves facilitated negotiation; negotiation is direct party-to-party resolution.
Uniform Resource Locator (URL) or file path to the digitally stored executed sales contract document. Links to document management system or secure repository.
Date when the sales contract becomes legally binding and enforceable. Marks the start of the contract period and triggers obligations for both parties.
Date when the sales contract ends and obligations cease. Nullable for open-ended contracts or contracts with indefinite terms subject to termination clauses.

Contractual provisions excusing non-performance due to unforeseeable events beyond parties control such as natural disasters, war, pandemics, or government actions. Defines qualifying events and relief procedures.
Legal jurisdiction and body of law that governs the interpretation, enforcement, and dispute resolution of the sales contract. Typically specifies country and state/province.

International Commercial Terms defining the responsibilities, costs, and risks between buyer and seller for delivery of goods. Examples: EXW (Ex Works), FOB (Free On Board), CIF (Cost Insurance and Freight), DDP (Delivered Duty Paid). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Mandatory insurance coverage that the supplier or customer must maintain during contract performance. May include general liability, professional indemnity, product liability, or cargo insurance with specified coverage limits.

Provisions defining ownership, licensing, and usage rights for intellectual property including patents, designs, software, documentation, and technical know-how. Specifies IP ownership and license grants.
Maximum financial liability amount that either party can be held responsible for under the sales contract. Limits exposure for damages, losses, or claims arising from contract performance.
Timestamp when this sales contract record was last updated in the system. Part of audit trail for change tracking and compliance.
Net monetary value of the sales contract after applying discounts, rebates, and before taxes. Represents the actual revenue to be recognized.
Free-text field for additional comments, special instructions, or contextual information about the sales contract. Used for internal communication and knowledge transfer.
Reference to the sales opportunity or deal that resulted in this executed contract. Links to CRM opportunity data for pipeline tracking and conversion analysis.

Instrument or mechanism through which the customer will remit payment. Wire transfer for electronic bank transfers; letter of credit for trade finance; bank guarantee for secured payments; check for paper-based payments; credit card for card payments; direct debit for automated withdrawals.
Detailed description of payment conditions including milestones, due dates, installment schedules, and payment triggers. Examples: Net 30, 50% upfront 50% on delivery, milestone-based payments.
Contractual provisions defining financial penalties or liquidated damages for non-performance, late delivery, or breach of SLA commitments. Specifies penalty amounts, triggers, and caps.
Reference to the formal quotation or proposal that was accepted and converted into this sales contract. Links to quote master data for pricing and terms traceability.
Provisions governing automatic renewal, renewal options, or renegotiation terms at contract expiration. Defines renewal notice periods, pricing adjustments, and renewal conditions.
Date when the sales contract was signed by both parties and became legally executed. May differ from effective date if contract has a future start date.

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Resolution Time Hours
description: Maximum number of hours within which the supplier must resolve customer issues or restore service under the contract. Part of SLA commitments for uptime and availability.

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Response Time Hours
description: Maximum number of hours within which the supplier must respond to customer service requests or incidents under the contract. Part of SLA commitments for support and maintenance.
Guaranteed minimum uptime or availability percentage for systems, equipment, or services covered by the sales contract. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 99.5% uptime).
Full name of the authorized representative who signed the sales contract on behalf of the supplier. Used for legal verification and audit trail.
Total tax amount applicable to the sales contract. Includes VAT, GST, sales tax, or other applicable taxes based on jurisdiction and product classification.
Contractual provisions defining conditions, notice periods, and procedures under which either party may terminate the sales contract. Includes termination for cause, convenience, or force majeure.
Total monetary value of the sales contract representing the gross revenue commitment. Includes all line items, products, services, and deliverables before adjustments.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the contract value. Defines the currency in which all financial terms, payments, and penalties are denominated.
Duration in months for which the supplier provides warranty coverage for products, systems, or equipment delivered under the sales contract. Covers defects in materials and workmanship.
Detailed description of warranty provisions including coverage scope, exclusions, repair or replacement terms, and customer obligations. Defines what is covered and what is not under warranty.
Primary key for campaign
Internal user responsible for the campaign execution and oversight.
Self-referencing FK on campaign (parent_campaign_id)
Number of clicks recorded for the campaign.
Number of times the campaign content was actually displayed.
Number of qualified leads actually generated.
Revenue realized from the campaign.
Total amount actually spent on the campaign to date.
Approved monetary budget allocated for the campaign.
External code or reference number assigned to the campaign, often used in marketing systems.
Detailed narrative describing the campaign purpose, messaging, and scope.
Human‑readable name of the campaign used in reports and dashboards.
Current lifecycle state of the campaign.
Primary channel through which the campaign is delivered (e.g., email, social media, search, event, direct).
Timestamp when the campaign record was first created in the system.
Date on which the campaign is scheduled to end or be retired.
Projected number of clicks generated by the campaign.
Projected number of times the campaign content will be displayed to the target audience.
Projected number of qualified leads generated by the campaign.
Projected revenue attributable to the campaign.
How often the campaign is executed or repeated.
True if the campaign is a test or pilot; false otherwise.
Two‑letter ISO language code of the primary language used in campaign assets.
Strategic objective the campaign aims to achieve.
Business priority level assigned to the campaign.
Three‑letter ISO code representing the primary geographic region targeted by the campaign.
Date on which the campaign is scheduled to begin.
Key performance indicator used to evaluate campaign success (e.g., leads_generated, revenue_attributed).
Description of the primary audience segment(s) the campaign is intended for.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the campaign record.
Unique identifier for the channel partner record. Primary key.
Identifier of the user who created this partner record.
Identifier of the internal employee (partner account manager or channel manager) responsible for managing the relationship with this partner.
Identifier of the user who last modified this partner record.
Identifier of the sales territory to which this partner is assigned for reporting and commission purposes.
The annual revenue target or quota assigned to the partner for the current fiscal year, in the partner's operating currency.

Comma-separated list of product lines or business units the partner is authorized to sell or integrate. Examples: Industrial Automation, Electrification, Building Technologies, Motion Control, Process Instrumentation.
Date when the partner's current certification expires and requires renewal.

Current status of the partner's technical or sales certification. Certified (all required certifications current), In Progress (certification training underway), Expired (certifications lapsed), Not Certified (no certifications obtained).
Expiration or renewal date of the current partner agreement or contract.
Effective start date of the current partner agreement or contract.
Timestamp when this partner record was first created in the system.
Maximum credit limit extended to the partner for outstanding receivables, in the partner's operating currency.
Indicates whether the partner is authorized to register deals for protection and special pricing. True if deal registration is enabled, False otherwise.
Discount tier assigned to the partner based on volume commitments and tier status. Determines the standard discount percentage the partner receives on product pricing.
Nine-digit Dun & Bradstreet DUNS number uniquely identifying the partner organization globally.
Size classification of the partner organization by number of employees.
Geographic territories or regions where the partner is authorized to operate and sell. May include country codes, state/province lists, or named territories.
First line of the street address for the partner's headquarters or principal place of business.
City where the partner's headquarters is located.
Three-letter ISO country code for the partner's headquarters location.
Postal or ZIP code for the partner's headquarters address.
State, province, or region where the partner's headquarters is located.
Primary industry verticals or market segments the partner specializes in serving. Examples: Automotive, Food & Beverage, Pharmaceuticals, Oil & Gas, Water & Wastewater.
Timestamp when this partner record was last updated or modified.
Date of the most recent formal business review or performance evaluation conducted with the partner.
The official registered legal name of the channel partner organization as it appears on incorporation documents and contracts.
Indicates whether the partner is eligible to receive Market Development Funds for co-marketing activities. True if eligible, False otherwise.
Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or comments about the partner relationship.
Externally-known unique business identifier for the channel partner, typically assigned by the partner management system. Used in contracts, deal registrations, and commercial correspondence.
Date when the partner was enrolled in the channel partner program and the relationship was established.

Current lifecycle status of the partner relationship. Active (authorized to transact), Inactive (temporarily not transacting), Suspended (authorization revoked pending review), Pending Approval (application under review), Terminated (relationship ended).

Partner program tier indicating authorization level, benefits, and performance status. Platinum (top tier, highest revenue and certification), Gold (high performance), Silver (standard), Bronze (entry level), Registered (basic enrollment).

Classification of the channel partner by business model: distributor (bulk stock and resell), system integrator (solution design and implementation), VAR (Value-Added Reseller - customize and resell), OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer - embed products), reseller (direct resale), consultant (advisory and referral).
Standard payment terms extended to the partner, expressed in number of days (e.g., 30, 60, 90 days net).
Primary email address for the main business contact at the partner organization. Used for operational communications, deal notifications, and program updates.
Primary telephone number for the main business contact at the partner organization.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the annual revenue target.
Tax identification number, VAT number, or equivalent government-issued business tax identifier for the partner organization.

Assessment of the partner's technical capability and expertise. Advanced (complex system integration, custom engineering), Intermediate (standard configuration and deployment), Basic (product resale with limited technical support).

business_glossary_term: Trade Name or Doing Business As (DBA)
description: The commercial trading name or DBA name under which the partner operates in the market, if different from legal name.
Primary website URL for the partner organization.
Unique identifier for the sales opportunity record. Primary key.
Reference to the marketing campaign that influenced or generated this opportunity.
Reference to the resulting contract or order if the opportunity was won and converted.
Reference to the customer account associated with this opportunity.
Reference to the primary contact person at the customer account for this opportunity.
Reference to the sales representative or user who owns and is responsible for this opportunity.
Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_project. Business justification: New product opportunities trigger an engineering project; linking enables tracking of project initiation and status.
Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_register. Business justification: Enables Opportunity Management to record the exact equipment to be sold, required for quote generation and project planning.
Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Opportunity tracking of a specific material enables demand forecasting and aligns inventory planning.

Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Opportunity stores territory as free text; adding opportunity_territory_id creates a proper link to sales.territory, enabling consistent reporting and removing redundant string column.
Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Opportunity reporting by product family is essential for portfolio performance analysis and strategic sales planning.
Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Opportunity stage forecasts profit per profit center, supporting sales‑finance alignment.
Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Project Execution Planning: tracks which project fulfills a won opportunity, essential for schedule and cost alignment.

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Opportunity qualification requires checking applicable regulatory requirements for the product line to ensure legal sellability.
Foreign key linking to sales.rep. Business justification: Opportunity is owned by a sales rep; adding sales_rep_id FK enables proper ownership tracking.

Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Opportunity pipeline and forecasting require linking each opportunity to the exact SKU being pursued, enabling accurate demand planning and revenue projection.
Reference to the quote that is currently synced with the opportunity amount and line items.
Total estimated revenue value of the opportunity in the base currency. Represents the gross deal size before discounts.
Target date by which the opportunity is expected to close (win or loss decision).
Actual date when the opportunity was marked as closed (won or lost). Null if still open.
Name of the primary competitor identified in this sales opportunity.
Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the opportunity location.
Timestamp when the opportunity record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the opportunity amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).
Current status of product delivery and installation for won opportunities. Tracks post-sale fulfillment.
Total discount percentage applied to the opportunity amount. Used for pricing and margin analysis.
Probability-weighted revenue calculated as amount multiplied by probability percentage. Used for forecast accuracy.
Fiscal quarter in which the opportunity is expected to close (e.g., Q1 FY2024). Used for quota and forecast alignment.
Fiscal year in which the opportunity is expected to close. Used for annual revenue planning.
Sales forecast classification indicating confidence level for revenue planning and quota tracking.
Boolean indicator whether there are open tasks or activities associated with this opportunity. Used for pipeline health monitoring.
Target industry vertical or market segment for this opportunity (e.g., automotive, food and beverage, pharmaceuticals).
Boolean indicator whether the opportunity has reached a final closed state (won or lost).
Boolean indicator whether the opportunity is marked as private with restricted visibility.
Boolean indicator whether the opportunity was closed as won (deal successfully secured).
Date of the most recent sales activity (call, meeting, email) logged against this opportunity.
Timestamp when the opportunity record was last updated.
Original source or channel through which the opportunity was generated.
Primary reason why the opportunity was lost. Used for win/loss analysis and competitive intelligence.
Description of the next planned action or milestone to advance the opportunity.
